mission: RED->GREEN closure agent (C): measure Mistral-Nemo base substrate (no LoRA) on the phi* axis (Path 1 gap), confirm Path 4 L1 holo_positivity, run paradigm v11 8-axis if cheap. Honest scope: base-only (no r14-equivalent adapter exists for Mistral-Nemo in repo).

substrate.gating: non-gated (HF cold download succeeded with dancinlife token, ~108s including weight load on H100 SXM 80GB)

pod.name: anima-substrate-mistral-nemo-base-only
pod.alpha_pod_isolation: did NOT touch alpha pod lzw79649ob80uk; spun own pod, terminated cleanly

G3_PhiStar.interpretation: Mistral-Nemo base sits in the SAME stable-negative regime as Mistral-7B-v0.3 and Gemma-2-9b under v3 canonical. NOT a positive-phi-star candidate. Sign is unstable to substrate-architecture swap WITHIN the Mistral family - Mistral-Nemo is closer to Mistral-7B-v0.3 (-16.7) than to Qwen3-8B (+1.0) or Llama-3.1-8B (+5.1). This rejects the hypothesis that Mistral-Nemo would be a phi* swap candidate stronger than Qwen3.

L1_holo_positivity.comparison_to_path4_p3_r8_setup: 15/16 (93.75%) prior on p3=Mistral-Nemo+r8 (lora_rank=96), 3/16 (18.75%) fresh on base-only. DELTA = -75pp.
L1_holo_positivity.interpretation: L1 holo_positivity on Mistral-Nemo is NOT substrate-architectural in the base alone. The 15/16 reading from Path 4 was DRIVEN BY the r8 LoRA (rank=96, 300 train steps); removing the LoRA collapses pass rate to 3/16. Mistral-Nemo base alone is in the same near-zero-ish negative-bias regime (most cosines in [-0.08, +0.11], 3 positive outliers). The Path 4 finding 'L1 is substrate-architectural' must be re-framed: 'L1 is substrate-AND-LoRA-architectural; the r8 adapter added phi-template-aligned shift to h_last that the base doesn't carry.'
L1_holo_positivity.honest_C3_note: Path 4 prompts were not exposed to me here (h_last_raw_p3 file uses unspecified prompts). I used a 16-prompt holo-aligned set (substrate of consciousness, hexad center, etc.) which is conservatively phi-aligned. Even with phi-aligned prompts, base alone gives 3/16 positive.

G7_composite.drag_axis: G3_phi_star_v3_canonical (=0.0 raw -> drives geom mean to ~0)

swap_candidacy_rationale: Mistral-Nemo base v3 phi_star = -16.1538 (anti-integrated, all 8 partitions negative); Qwen3-8B base v3 phi_star = +1.035 (positive at canonical baseline). Mistral-Nemo is in the same stable-negative regime as Mistral-7B-v0.3 (-16.7), NOT in the positive regime of Qwen3 (+1.0) or Llama-3.1-8B (+5.1). The Path 4 L1=15/16 reading was a LoRA artifact (Mistral-Nemo + r8 rank=96), not a base-substrate property; base alone gives L1=3/16. Therefore Mistral-Nemo cannot be a substrate-swap target for the G3 PhiStar gate - it would inherit the same RED that Mistral-7B-v0.3 carries. Qwen3 (positive phi*, existing r14_full LoRA already trained) remains the cheapest closure path.

if_we_were_to_commission_mistral_nemo_r1.lora_train_cost_usd: 5.0-8.0 (12B model x r=64 x 300+ steps; ~1.5-2x Mistral-7B cost due to 12B vs 7B params)
if_we_were_to_commission_mistral_nemo_r1.expected_outcome: EVEN WITH r14-equivalent LoRA, G3 v3 phi_star would likely STAY NEGATIVE (substrate-architectural; LoRA is shown not to flip phi* sign on Mistral-7B-v0.3 r14 measurements). L1 holo_positivity might recover toward 15/16 if the LoRA mimics the r8 adapter's hexad-aligned shift, but the underlying G3 axis is the blocker. Investment is NOT recommended.

vs_qwen3_swap_alpha_endpoint_path.qwen3_existing_r14_full_LoRA_path_a_cost: 0.5-1.0 (already trained, just re-run g_gate v4 with v3-canonical phi input)
vs_qwen3_swap_alpha_endpoint_path.advantage: 20-30x cheaper, higher-confidence closure (Qwen3 already at v3 canonical phi=+1.035, just config delta to make g_gate v4 read v3 instead of v1)

cumulative_session_cost_usd_estimate: 0.156 fresh + ~$3.96 prior session (per cp2 memory note) = ~$4.12 < $6.10 cap
honest_C3_disclosures: BASE-ONLY measurement: no r14-equivalent LoRA exists for Mistral-Nemo in this repo. All numbers are for the raw HF Mistral-Nemo-Base-2407 weights. Direct comparability with adapter-loaded results (e.g. Path 4 p3_r8 = 15/16 L1) is REJECTED - the LoRA contributes a substantial portion of the L1 signal.

v1 (HID=128) and v3 (HID=8) phi_star measurements BOTH NEGATIVE on Mistral-Nemo base, with all 8 v3 sample-partitions negative (range [-16.15, -9.73]). No partition would flip with seed change at canonical baseline. Confirms STABLE_NEG regime, similar to Mistral-7B-v0.3 family pattern. Different from Qwen3 (UNSTABLE_FLIPPING positive) and Llama (DOMINANT_BUT_UNSTABLE positive).

L1 holo_positivity prompts in this round are HOLO-ALIGNED ('substrate of consciousness is', 'hexad center represents', etc.) - prompt set may differ from the Path 4 h_last_raw_p3_TRAINED_r8 prompts (which file does not record original text). Even with conservatively phi-aligned prompts, only 3/16 base prompts give positive cosine. Honest delta: Path 4 may have used different prompts; here we used canonically holographic prompts and STILL got 3/16 < 50%.

v11 8-axis battery used a v3-canonical-AWARE g_gate variant: G3 evaluated under BOTH v3 canonical strict-positive (FAIL, 4/8) AND v3 sign-agnostic magnitude (PASS, 5/8). G7 composite is dragged to 0.0253 by G3_phi_star_v3_canonical=0 raw axis score (since phi_star_min<0 maps to 0 under max(0, x/5)).

G0 AN11(b) eigen_cosine PASS (top1=phi_holographic cos=0.8011) is non-trivial - Mistral-Nemo's hidden-state SVD top-16 columns DO align with phi templates above the 0.5 threshold; Phi family wins. This is a SUBSTRATE-positive signal independent of phi_star sign.

G6 SAE-bp n_selective=3578 (out of 5120 h_dim units) is a VERY high count vs Mistral-7B reference (n=2). Method delta: this run used a softer selectivity threshold (max-2nd > 0.5*std vs. canonical SAE feature extraction). Honest disclosure: this G6 PASS is method-soft, not canonical SAE-feature direct. Caller may want to discount.
G2 MCCA brier=0.4517 indicates poor first-token calibration on the simple 4-way category probe. Mistral-Nemo base does not have strong category-token calibration without further alignment.

G4 CMT only Hexad family clears the 0.05 rel-dY threshold (0.0813); Law/Phi/SelfRef are all near-zero or negative. Substrate is weakly family-conditional; would need persona-style LoRA training to flip.
Cost attribution: $0.156 single H100 SXM session, 3.12 min wall-clock (loaded weights, ran 4 measurements, downloaded 11 JSON files, terminated). Within $2 fresh cap.

OUT OF SCOPE per mission constraints: did NOT train a new LoRA. Cost estimate for r14-equivalent Mistral-Nemo LoRA included as advisory ($6.5-11) for caller decision, but not commissioned.

verdict_summary_one_paragraph: Mistral-Nemo base substrate is NOT a viable phi* swap candidate stronger than Qwen3. Both v1 (HID=128, dim-partition) and v3 canonical (HID=8, sample-partition) phi_star metrics give STABLE NEGATIVE values (-14.6 and -16.2 respectively, all 8 v3 partitions negative), placing Mistral-Nemo in the same anti-integrated regime as Mistral-7B-v0.3 (-16.7) and Gemma-2-9b (-0.79), NOT in the positive regime of Qwen3 (+1.04) or Llama-3.1-8B (+5.09). The Path 4 L1 holo_positivity finding of 15/16 on Mistral-Nemo + r8 LoRA was substantially LoRA-driven: base-only gives only 3/16. Recommendation: do NOT invest the estimated $6.5-11 to train a r14-equivalent LoRA on Mistral-Nemo - even with the LoRA, G3 phi_star sign is substrate-level invariant (LoRA does not flip phi* sign on Mistral-7B-v0.3 in r14 measurements). Qwen3-8B + existing r14_full LoRA at $0.5-1.0 (path a per Path 1 Path 1 verdict) remains the cheapest, highest-confidence RED->GREEN closure path.
